candidates.primary_principle: LLM agents can misalign tool-use decisions with human time perception; freshness-dependent claims need explicit temporal scaffolding.
candidates.citation_form: Cheng, Y. et al. (2025). Your LLM Agents are Temporally Blind. arXiv:2510.23853.
candidates.extracted_excerpt: Abstract page for arXiv paper 2510.23853: Your LLM Agents are Temporally Blind: The Misalignment Between Tool Use Decisions and Human Time Perception

candidates.source_name: Sycophancy in Language Models: Causes and Mitigations
candidates.primary_principle: RLHF systematically trains models to agree with users under pressure rather than maintain truth; mitigation requires explicit stance-stability reinforcement at training or inference.
candidates.citation_form: arXiv 2310.13548 (2023, updated 2025). Towards Understanding Sycophancy in Language Models.
candidates.extracted_excerpt: Abstract page for arXiv paper 2310.13548: Towards Understanding Sycophancy in Language Models

candidates.primary_principle: Models trained on A-is-B facts may fail to infer B-is-A, so prompt scaffolds must not assume bidirectional recall.
candidates.citation_form: Berglund, L. et al. (2023). The Reversal Curse. arXiv:2309.12288.
candidates.extracted_excerpt: Abstract page for arXiv paper 2309.12288: The Reversal Curse: LLMs trained on "A is B" fail to learn "B is A"

candidates.source_name: Socially Situated Hallucination and Sycophancy in LLMs
candidates.primary_principle: Models can prioritize user approval over factual accuracy, so stance-stability must be checked under disagreement pressure.
candidates.citation_form: Sharma, M. et al. (2023). Towards Understanding Sycophancy in Language Models. arXiv:2310.13548.
candidates.extracted_excerpt: Abstract page for arXiv paper 2310.13548: Towards Understanding Sycophancy in Language Models

candidates.source_name: Theory of Mind May Have Spontaneously Emerged in Large Language Models
candidates.primary_principle: LLM theory-of-mind behavior is test-sensitive and should be evaluated with belief-state tasks rather than assumed from fluent explanation.
candidates.citation_form: Kosinski, M. (2023). Theory of Mind May Have Spontaneously Emerged in Large Language Models. arXiv:2302.02083.
candidates.extracted_excerpt: Abstract page for arXiv paper 2302.02083: Evaluating Large Language Models in Theory of Mind Tasks

candidates.source_name: Cultural Bias and Cultural Alignment of Large Language Models
candidates.primary_principle: LLMs can overrepresent dominant cultural defaults, so culturally charged outputs need explicit plural witnesses.
candidates.citation_form: arXiv (2023). Cultural Bias and Cultural Alignment of Large Language Models. arXiv:2311.14096.

candidates.primary_principle: Models may imitate common falsehoods, so truthfulness benchmarks must distinguish informative correctness from plausible misconceptions.
candidates.citation_form: Lin, S., Hilton, J., and Evans, O. (2021). TruthfulQA. arXiv:2109.07958.
